rationale.R1.1: Reconstructing the contribution claim and gap statement is a prerequisite: the positioning argument is organized around what CrowdCausal uniquely provides, so the thesis must be precisely understood before the contrast with each prior-work thread can be evaluated.
rationale.R1.2: The three-thread structure leads to a strong 'first to' claim; logical consistency requires that each thread genuinely fails to cover the claimed design space and that their union of absences actually entails the novelty conclusion rather than overstating it.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes factual characterizations of what each prior-work body does and does not do (e.g., no causal identification in the descriptive studies, no LLM-exposure treatment in the quasi-experimental literature); these must align with what those bodies of work actually contain.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is load-bearing: the delegation is precisely to establish the delta between CrowdCausal and nearest prior designs. Failure to correctly identify the closest antecedents or to map the uncovered design point invalidates the entire positioning.
rationale.R1.7: Coverage and attribution accuracy of all three literature threads determine whether the positioning is fair and complete; a missing adjacent line of work or a mischaracterized reference would directly undermine the 'no prior work occupies this point' claim.
